description: Add comprehensive SDK utilities to @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk that reduce boilerplate for plugin authors. The utilities cover DOM querying/waiting, page fetch with error handling, storage access, page state introspection, and retry/timing patterns. All utilities are generic (not tied to any specific plugin), exported from the SDK's public API, unit tested, and validated end-to-end through the e2e-test plugin. After implementation, the SDK is published to npm and plugins are updated to use the new version.

userStories.title: Add DOM utilities to plugin SDK
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I want DOM utility functions in the SDK so I can wait for elements, query the DOM, and observe mutations without writing boilerplate MutationObserver/polling code in every plugin.
acceptanceCriteria: sdk.waitForSelector(selector, opts?) returns Promise<Element> — polls with MutationObserver, configurable timeout (default 10s), rejects with descriptive error on timeout
sdk.waitForSelectorRemoval(selector, opts?) returns Promise<void> — resolves when the element matching selector is removed from the DOM, configurable timeout (default 10s)
sdk.querySelectorAll<T>(selector) returns T[] — typed wrapper around document.querySelectorAll, returns a real array (not NodeList)
sdk.getTextContent(selector) returns string | null — returns trimmed textContent of the first matching element, or null if not found

sdk.observeDOM(selector, callback, options?) returns () => void — sets up MutationObserver on matching element, returns cleanup function; options include childList, attributes, subtree (defaults: childList+subtree true)
All functions are exported from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk (platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts re-exports them)
Unit tests in platform/plugin-sdk/src/dom.test.ts using happy-dom for DOM simulation
bun run build, bun run type-check, bun run lint, bun run knip, bun run test all pass
userStories.notes: Implementation file: platform/plugin-sdk/src/dom.ts. Export from platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts (currently 97 lines, add re-exports at the bottom). The SDK currently has one test file: platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.test.ts (101 lines) — create a new dom.test.ts alongside it. The tsconfig.test.json at platform/plugin-sdk/tsconfig.test.json already includes 'src/**/*.test.ts' so new test files are auto-discovered. For happy-dom: add 'happy-dom' as a devDependency in platform/plugin-sdk/package.json. Use globalThis.document and globalThis.MutationObserver from happy-dom in tests. waitForSelector implementation: create a MutationObserver on document.body watching childList+subtree, check document.querySelector on each mutation, resolve on match, reject on timeout via setTimeout. waitForSelectorRemoval: similar pattern but resolve when querySelector returns null. observeDOM: querySelector the target, attach MutationObserver, return disconnect function. Make sure to add the new dom.ts module to the tsconfig include paths (already covered by 'src' include in platform/plugin-sdk/tsconfig.json). Run 'bun run knip' to verify no unused exports.

userStories.title: Add page fetch utilities to plugin SDK
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I want typed fetch utilities in the SDK that use the page's cookies/session, with built-in error handling, timeout support, and JSON parsing, so I don't have to write the same boilerplate fetch-then-check-ok-then-parse-json pattern in every tool handler.
acceptanceCriteria: sdk.fetchFromPage(url, init?) returns Promise<Response> — uses native fetch with credentials:'include', configurable timeout via AbortSignal.timeout (default 30s), throws descriptive error on non-ok status including status code and response text
sdk.fetchJSON<T>(url, init?) returns Promise<T> — calls fetchFromPage, parses JSON, returns typed result; throws on parse failure with descriptive error
sdk.postJSON<T>(url, body, init?) returns Promise<T> — convenience for POST with JSON body (sets Content-Type, stringifies body), calls fetchJSON
All functions handle AbortSignal timeout, non-ok HTTP status, and JSON parse errors with clear error messages
All functions are exported from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk
Unit tests in platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.test.ts
bun run build, bun run type-check, bun run lint, bun run knip, bun run test all pass
userStories.notes: Implementation file: platform/plugin-sdk/src/fetch.ts. Export from platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts. These are NOT trivial one-liner wrappers — they add real value: (1) credentials:'include' by default (page session cookies), (2) configurable timeout via AbortSignal.timeout with a sensible default, (3) automatic non-ok status checking with error text extraction, (4) JSON parse error handling, (5) generic type parameter for type-safe responses. Compare with plugins/e2e-test/src/test-api.ts (44 lines) and plugins/slack/src/slack-api.ts lines 362-428 — both implement the same fetch+check-ok+parse-json+error-handling pattern manually. For unit tests: use Bun's built-in fetch mock or create a simple Bun.serve() test server in the test file. The fetch functions should throw ToolError (already exported from the SDK at platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts:88-97) for consistent error handling across plugins. The init parameter should accept standard RequestInit and merge with defaults (credentials, signal, headers).

userStories.title: Add storage utilities to plugin SDK
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I want simple typed wrappers for reading localStorage, sessionStorage, and cookies so I can access page state without writing try-catch boilerplate for each storage API.
acceptanceCriteria: sdk.getLocalStorage(key) returns string | null — wraps localStorage.getItem with try-catch (returns null on SecurityError or missing)
sdk.setLocalStorage(key, value) returns void — wraps localStorage.setItem with try-catch (silently fails on SecurityError)
sdk.getSessionStorage(key) returns string | null — wraps sessionStorage.getItem with try-catch
sdk.getCookie(name) returns string | null — parses document.cookie string, handles URI-encoded values, returns null if cookie not found
All functions are exported from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk
Unit tests in platform/plugin-sdk/src/storage.test.ts
bun run build, bun run type-check, bun run lint, bun run knip, bun run test all pass
userStories.notes: Implementation file: platform/plugin-sdk/src/storage.ts. Export from platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts. The try-catch wrappers are important because storage APIs can throw SecurityError in sandboxed iframes or when storage is disabled. See plugins/slack/src/slack-api.ts lines 35-58 (getAuthFromLocalStorage) — it wraps localStorage access in try-catch, which every plugin would need to do. getCookie implementation: split document.cookie by '; ', find the entry starting with 'name=', extract and decodeURIComponent the value. For unit tests: happy-dom provides localStorage and sessionStorage globals. For document.cookie, happy-dom supports it — set cookies directly in the test and read them back. Keep the implementation simple — these are thin safety wrappers, not full cookie-jar implementations.

userStories.title: Add page state utilities to plugin SDK
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I want utilities to safely read page globals, the current URL, and page title so I can introspect page state without writing repetitive null-checking and type-casting code.
acceptanceCriteria: sdk.getPageGlobal<T>(path) returns T | undefined — safe deep property access on globalThis using dot-notation path (e.g., 'TS.boot_data.team_id'), returns undefined if any segment is missing, wraps in try-catch
sdk.getCurrentUrl() returns string — returns window.location.href
sdk.getPageTitle() returns string — returns document.title
All functions are exported from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk
Unit tests in platform/plugin-sdk/src/page-state.test.ts
bun run build, bun run type-check, bun run lint, bun run knip, bun run test all pass
userStories.notes: Implementation file: platform/plugin-sdk/src/page-state.ts. Export from platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts. getPageGlobal is the most valuable utility here — see plugins/slack/src/slack-api.ts lines 111-149 (getAuthFromBootData) which manually traverses window.TS.boot_data, window.TS.model, window.TS.redux.getState() — all with defensive null checks and type casts. getPageGlobal('TS.boot_data.api_token') would replace 5+ lines of defensive access. Implementation: split path by '.', walk globalThis reducing each segment with optional chaining semantics, wrap in try-catch to handle getters that throw. Type parameter T is for the caller's convenience (cast at the call site, no runtime validation). For unit tests: set properties on globalThis in beforeEach/afterEach, clean up after. Test edge cases: missing intermediate properties, empty path, properties that are null vs undefined.

userStories.title: Add retry/timing utilities to plugin SDK
userStories.description: As a plugin author, I want retry, sleep, and waitUntil utilities so I can handle transient failures, implement polling, and wait for conditions without writing custom retry loops in every tool handler.
acceptanceCriteria: sdk.retry<T>(fn, opts?) returns Promise<T> — retries fn on failure with maxAttempts (default 3), delay (default 1000ms), optional exponential backoff (default false), optional AbortSignal support; re-throws last error after all attempts exhausted
sdk.sleep(ms) returns Promise<void> — simple promisified setTimeout
sdk.waitUntil(predicate, opts?) returns Promise<void> — polls predicate at interval (default 200ms), resolves when predicate returns true, rejects on timeout (default 10s) with descriptive error
All functions are exported from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk
Unit tests in platform/plugin-sdk/src/timing.test.ts
bun run build, bun run type-check, bun run lint, bun run knip, bun run test all pass
userStories.notes: Implementation file: platform/plugin-sdk/src/timing.ts. Export from platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts. See plugins/slack/src/slack-api.ts lines 329-346 (waitForSlackAuth) — a hand-rolled polling loop with interval and timeout. sdk.waitUntil would replace this entirely. Also see the Slack API call at line 398 which uses AbortSignal.timeout(30_000) — the retry utility should support AbortSignal for cancellation. retry implementation: loop maxAttempts times, call fn in try-catch, on error sleep(delay * backoffMultiplier), check signal.aborted before retrying. waitUntil implementation: setInterval + setTimeout combo, clear both on resolution. sleep implementation: new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, ms)). For unit tests: use Bun's test framework with mock timers if available, or use real timers with short intervals (10-50ms). Test retry with functions that fail N times then succeed, test abort signal cancellation, test waitUntil with predicates that become true after a delay.

userStories.title: Add E2E tests for SDK utilities using e2e-test plugin
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want E2E tests that verify SDK utilities work end-to-end through the full dispatch chain (MCP client → MCP server → Chrome extension → page context → SDK utility → response) so I can be confident the utilities work in real browser contexts.
acceptanceCriteria: The e2e-test plugin uses SDK utilities in existing or new tools (replace manual DOM/fetch code with SDK calls where applicable)

New E2E test tools exercise: waitForSelector (wait for a DOM element that appears after a delay), fetchJSON (call the test server API through SDK), getLocalStorage (read a value set by the test page), getPageGlobal (read a window global set by the test page), retry (call a flaky endpoint that fails then succeeds)
New E2E test cases in e2e/ verify the SDK utilities work through the full dispatch chain
The test server (e2e/test-server.ts) is extended with any necessary endpoints (e.g., a flaky endpoint for retry testing, a page that sets localStorage/globals)
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
This story requires updating multiple files:

1. Test server (e2e/test-server.ts, 546 lines): Add new endpoints and page features:
   - Add a '/sdk-test' page route that serves HTML with: (a) a delayed element (appears after 500ms via setTimeout), (b) localStorage.setItem('sdk-test-key', 'sdk-test-value'), (c) window.__sdkTestGlobal = { nested: { value: 42 } }, (d) an element with known textContent for getTextContent testing.
   - Add 'POST /api/flaky' endpoint that fails the first N requests then succeeds (controlled by a counter in state, resettable via /control/reset). This tests sdk.retry.
   - Add 'POST /api/sdk-fetch-test' endpoint that returns { ok: true, data: 'sdk-fetch-works' } for testing fetchJSON.

2. E2E test plugin (plugins/e2e-test/): Add new tool files in plugins/e2e-test/src/tools/:
   - sdk-wait-for-selector.ts: uses sdk.waitForSelector('#delayed-element') on the /sdk-test page
   - sdk-fetch-json.ts: uses sdk.fetchJSON<T>('/api/sdk-fetch-test') instead of manual fetch
   - sdk-get-local-storage.ts: uses sdk.getLocalStorage('sdk-test-key')
   - sdk-get-page-global.ts: uses sdk.getPageGlobal<number>('__sdkTestGlobal.nested.value')
   - sdk-retry.ts: uses sdk.retry(() => sdk.fetchJSON('/api/flaky'), { maxAttempts: 5, delay: 100 })
   Register all new tools in plugins/e2e-test/src/index.ts (line 17, tools array).
   Rebuild: cd plugins/e2e-test && bun install && bun run build.

3. E2E test file: Create e2e/sdk-utilities.e2e.ts with test cases that:
   - Navigate to the /sdk-test page URL on the test server
   - Call each SDK tool via mcpClient.callTool() and verify the response
   - Use the existing fixture pattern from e2e/fixtures.ts (mcpServer, testServer, extensionContext, mcpClient fixtures)
   - Follow the pattern in e2e/tool-dispatch.e2e.ts for reference

4. The e2e-test plugin must first be updated to depend on the new SDK version with the utility functions (from US-001 through US-005). Since this is before publishing (US-007), use the local path or build the SDK locally. Actually — the e2e-test plugin depends on the npm-published @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk. For E2E testing before publish, you have two options: (a) temporarily use 'file:../../platform/plugin-sdk' in the e2e-test plugin's package.json dependencies, build, test, then revert before US-007, or (b) do US-007 (publish) first, then come back. Option (a) is preferred — use file: protocol for local testing, then US-007 will switch to the published version.

5. Update the test server's PAGE_HTML or add the /sdk-test page. The existing /interactive page (lines 216-279) shows how to serve custom HTML pages — follow the same pattern.

6. For the fixtures, the config created by createTestConfigDir() (fixtures.ts line 127) reads tool names from plugins/e2e-test/dist/tools.json (line 122-125). After adding new tools and rebuilding the plugin, the new tools will automatically be included in the test config.

userStories.title: Publish updated SDK and update plugins
userStories.description: As a platform maintainer, I want to publish the updated @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk (and its dependencies) to npm so that plugins can depend on the published version with the new SDK utilities.
acceptanceCriteria: @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk version is bumped (e.g., from 0.0.10 to 0.0.11)
All platform packages are published in dependency order: shared → plugin-sdk → plugin-tools → cli
npm propagation is verified (retry 'npm view @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk version' with delays, do NOT assume token expired on 404/403)
plugins/e2e-test/package.json updated to depend on the new @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk version
plugins/slack/package.json updated to depend on the new @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk version
Both plugins build successfully: cd plugins/<name> && bun install && bun run build
All platform quality gates pass: bun run build, bun run type-check, bun run lint, bun run knip, bun run test
userStories.notes:
Use the existing publish script at scripts/publish.sh (100 lines). Current version is 0.0.10 (see platform/plugin-sdk/package.json line 3). Bump to the next appropriate version.

Publish steps:
1. Run 'bun run build' to ensure everything compiles.
2. Run './scripts/publish.sh <new-version>' — this bumps versions in all platform packages (platform/shared, platform/plugin-sdk, platform/plugin-tools, platform/cli), updates cross-references, rebuilds, swaps to publish token (~/.npmrc.publish → ~/.npmrc), and publishes all 4 packages in dependency order.
3. IMPORTANT: After publishing, there can be npm registry propagation delays (30s-2min). Retry 'npm view @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk@<version> version' with 10-second delays, up to 12 attempts. Do NOT interpret 404 or E403 as a token/auth problem during this window — it's propagation lag.
4. Once the new version is visible on npm, update plugin dependencies:
   - plugins/e2e-test/package.json line 39: change '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk' version
   - plugins/e2e-test/package.json line 42: change '@opentabs-dev/plugin-tools' version
   - plugins/slack/package.json line 37: change '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk' version
   - plugins/slack/package.json line 40: change '@opentabs-dev/plugin-tools' version
5. Rebuild both plugins: cd plugins/e2e-test && bun install && bun run build && cd ../slack && bun install && bun run build
6. If the e2e-test plugin was temporarily using 'file:' protocol for local SDK testing in US-006, revert it to the published npm version now.
7. Run all quality gates from the repo root.

The ~/.npmrc token can do both read and write (per the user's note). The publish script handles token swapping automatically (lines 68-77 of scripts/publish.sh).

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md to document SDK utilities
userStories.description: As a developer (or AI agent) working on the OpenTabs codebase, I want CLAUDE.md to document the available SDK utility functions so I know what's available and how to use them when writing plugins.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md includes a new section documenting all SDK utility functions with brief descriptions and example usage

Documentation covers: DOM utilities (waitForSelector, waitForSelectorRemoval, querySelectorAll, getTextContent, observeDOM), fetch utilities (fetchFromPage, fetchJSON, postJSON), storage utilities (getLocalStorage, setLocalStorage, getSessionStorage, getCookie), page state utilities (getPageGlobal, getCurrentUrl, getPageTitle), and timing utilities (retry, sleep, waitUntil)
Each function's signature, return type, and purpose are described
CLAUDE.md remains plugin-agnostic (no plugin-specific examples)
CLAUDE.md is accurate and reflects the actual implementation
userStories.notes: Edit CLAUDE.md (root of the repo). Add a new section titled '### SDK Utilities' under the 'Key Concepts' area (after the existing concepts like plugin discovery, tool prefixing, tab state machine, dev vs production mode, hot reload). Keep the documentation concise — CLAUDE.md explicitly says 'Keep additions high-level — avoid excessive detail that wastes context'. Group utilities by category (DOM, Fetch, Storage, Page State, Timing). For each function, provide: function signature, return type, one-sentence description. Include one brief example per category showing how a plugin tool handler would use the utility. Do NOT list individual plugins or plugin-specific use cases — keep it generic per the CLAUDE.md guidelines. The section should be roughly 40-60 lines — enough to be informative without being a full API reference.
